summary: Well-scoped plan with minimal architectural impact. Opacity crossfade is the correct approach given incompatible geometries across the three systems. Changes are confined to the visual layer with no audio or input pipeline modifications.

interface_changes.description: Optional setOpacity method added to GeometrySystem interface
interface_changes.notes: Optional method preserves backward compatibility. No existing consumers break.

shader_changes.description: New uOpacity uniform added to both fragment shaders
shader_changes.notes: Single uniform addition with multiplication into existing alpha output. Both shaders are currently identical; plan maintains this symmetry.

state_management.description: Transition state machine added to modeManager
state_management.notes: modeManager grows from simple index-based delegation to a two-phase state machine (idle vs transitioning). This is the largest change but is well-contained — renderLoop.ts and scene.ts are untouched.

rendering_pipeline.description: Two geometry systems rendered simultaneously during transition
rendering_pipeline.notes: Both systems already use AdditiveBlending + transparent:true + depthWrite:false. Overlapping two additive-blended point clouds produces a natural visual blend. GPU cost is bounded by existing quality scaling (600-1200 points max per system).

file_accuracy.notes: All 8 files identified are correct. renderLoop.ts is correctly excluded since modeManager.draw() already wraps system delegation.

details: Correctly identifies that pointWarp.frag.glsl and ribbonWarp.frag.glsl both use hardcoded 'alpha * 0.85' with no opacity uniform
Correctly identifies that ParticleField uses PointsMaterial with static opacity: 0.9, controllable via material.opacity
Correctly identifies that PointCloud and RibbonField use custom ShaderMaterial requiring a uniform-based approach
Correctly identifies the instant-swap pattern in modeManager (cleanup + init at time boundary)
Correctly identifies the seeded PRNG pattern (seed + ':mode') for deterministic transition duration
switchInterval range in plan (2000-4000ms for transition) is distinct from mode duration (90000-180000ms)

crossfade_or_morph.how: Opacity crossfade with both systems rendering simultaneously during transition. Outgoing fades from 1→0, incoming fades from 0→1.

transition_duration_seeded.how: Duration derived from existing PRNG: 2000 + floor(rng() * 2000), giving 2-4 second range.

no_frame_hitches.how: Incoming system is initialized before transition starts. During transition, only uniform updates occur — no object creation or disposal. Outgoing cleanup happens only after fade completes.

audio_continues_uninterrupted.how: Audio pipeline is entirely decoupled in renderLoop.ts. No audio code is modified. Both systems receive bassEnergy and trebleEnergy via shared FrameState during transition.

test_coverage.notes: Tests T-035-01 through T-035-08 cover crossfade mechanics, seeded duration, cleanup timing, no-throw guarantee, audio continuity, state observability, opacity values, and integration. Existing tests T-026-17, T-026-22, T-030-23 correctly identified as needing updates for transition-aware assertions.

risks.risk: GPU overdraw during transition with two full point clouds
risks.mitigation: Additive blending is already used. Point counts are quality-capped at 600-1200. Transition is 2-4 seconds. Negligible impact.

risks.risk: ParticleField base opacity (0.9) multiplication may produce unexpected visual weight
risks.mitigation: Plan correctly accounts for this with 'material.opacity = opacity * 0.9' formula. Visual result will be consistent with shader-based systems.
